title: Session Revocation API Endpoint
story: As a product administrator, I want to terminate user sessions on demand, so that I can immediately revoke access for compromised accounts or departing employees.
description:
Expose an API endpoint for administrators to revoke user sessions for security or compliance reasons.

## Endpoint

`POST /api/auth/revoke`

## Parameters

- `user_id` — revoke all sessions for a user
- `session_id` — revoke a specific session

## Use Cases

- Admin force-logout of a compromised account
- User-initiated "log out all devices"
- Account suspension or termination

## Technical Approach

- Wrap Stytch's `sessions.revoke()` method
- For "terminate all user sessions": call with `user_id`
- For single session: call with `session_id`
- Log revocation events with reason
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/auth/revoke endpoint is exposed and documented
Accepts user_id parameter to revoke all sessions for a user
Accepts session_id parameter to revoke a specific session
Returns success/failure response with appropriate status codes
Revocation events are logged with reason and admin context
middleware_changes: Session revocation service wrapping Stytch's sessions.revoke() with logging and error handling.
api_changes: POST /api/auth/revoke — accepts {user_id} or {session_id}, returns success/failure. Admin-only authorization required.
implementation_notes: Stytch handles the actual session invalidation — the endpoint is a thin wrapper with authorization checks, input validation, and audit logging.
